meaning: n.被发现的东西,收藏的东西
examples: The dictionary is a vast treasure trove of information.
McKee and his team now knew for certain that they had found the wreck, but were as yet unaware that it also housed a treasure trove of beautifully preserved artefacts.
Caldwell says the forces retrieved a treasure trove of documents that will help find other militants.
Getting the digital offer model right also opens a treasure trove of customer data.
Automated sharing is giving Facebook a treasure-trove of data, regardless of whether anyone cares.
